title: Karpathy: Train and inference GPT in 243 lines of pure Python
why_it_matters: Karpathy distills the full algorithmic content of GPT training and inference into 243 lines of dependency-free Python — no frameworks, no libraries. A masterclass in understanding what actually matters vs. what's just efficiency scaffolding.

title: Cursor's Third Era: Cloud Agents
why_it_matters: Cursor has acquired Graphite and Autotab and says Cloud Agents have overtaken its IDE use case, signaling that the $50B company sees the future beyond VS Code forks. This is a strategic shift the entire dev-tools ecosystem needs to watch.

title: Cursor's Third Era: Cloud Agents
why_it_matters: Cursor has acquired Graphite and Autotab and says Cloud Agents have overtaken its IDE use case, signaling the company sees the future beyond VS Code forks.

title: OpenAI reaches agreement with Department of War for classified network deployment
why_it_matters: Sam Altman announces OpenAI will deploy models in the DoW's classified network, while emphasizing prohibitions on domestic mass surveillance and offensive cyber operations. A landmark moment for AI in government — and a stark contrast to Anthropic's resistance.

title: Meta releases SAM Audio — isolate any sound with text, visual, or span prompts
why_it_matters: The first unified model that separates any sound from complex audio mixtures using multimodal prompts. Open-sourced with perception encoder, benchmarks, and papers. Opens up audio editing workflows that were previously impossible.
action: Try the model and perception encoder

title: Karpathy: CLIs are the killer interface for AI agents
why_it_matters: Karpathy argues CLIs are the ideal agent interface precisely because they're 'legacy' — deterministic, composable, and machine-readable. Pair GitHub CLI + Polymarket CLI + a coding agent and you get arbitrary dashboards built on demand.
action: Try pairing CLIs with your coding agent

title: GPT-5.2 derives new result in theoretical physics
why_it_matters: Follow-up: OpenAI releases a preprint with IAS, Vanderbilt, Cambridge, and Harvard showing GPT-5.2 discovered that a gluon interaction many physicists expected impossible can arise under specific conditions. First credible AI-derived result in fundamental physics.

title: Designing AI-resistant technical evaluations
why_it_matters: Anthropic's engineering team shares their framework for building technical interview questions and evaluations that remain meaningful when candidates have access to AI tools. Immediately useful for any hiring manager in 2026.

title: Claude Code Security: frontier cybersecurity for defenders
why_it_matters: Follow-up: Full blog post details on Claude Code Security's approach — scans codebases for vulnerabilities and generates targeted patches for human review. Positions Claude as a security tool, not just a coding assistant.

title: Perplexity releases pplx-embed-v1-0.6b embedding model
why_it_matters: Perplexity enters the embedding model space with a compact 0.6B model. At this size it's highly deployable for RAG and search pipelines, and comes from a team that lives and breathes retrieval quality.

title: Claude is a space to think
why_it_matters: Anthropic reframes Claude's product direction — not a chatbot, not a search engine, but a 'thinking space.' Signals a deliberate divergence from the ChatGPT/Gemini paradigm with implications for how developers should build on Claude.

title: LeCun shares proof that LLMs don't understand what they say
why_it_matters: Yann LeCun amplifies Valerio Capraro's experiment showing GPT gives contradictory moral judgments when question framing changes — e.g., judging the same action acceptable or unacceptable depending on how it's described. Adds empirical weight to the 'understanding vs. pattern matching' debate.

title: Your LLM doesn't write correct code — define acceptance criteria first
why_it_matters: Practical guide arguing that LLM code quality is primarily a prompting problem — define acceptance criteria, test cases, and constraints upfront, and correctness improves dramatically. Backed by real-world quantitative examples.
action: Add acceptance criteria to your prompts

title: LocoOperator-4B: lightweight agent model for GUI and tool use
why_it_matters: A 4B-parameter model specifically designed for agent/operator tasks — small enough to self-host, purpose-built for GUI interaction and tool calling. Fills the gap between giant operator models and local deployment needs.

title: AI Engineer will be the LAST job
why_it_matters: Latent Space reflects on the emerging consensus that AI engineering may be the last technical role standing as agents take over implementation. A useful framing for career planning and team structure decisions.

title: We might all be AI engineers now
why_it_matters: A developer's personal reckoning with the reality that every software role now involves AI engineering — whether you chose it or not. Practical perspective on how traditional backend/frontend distinctions are dissolving.
action: Assess your own AI skill gaps

title: Qwen3-14B distilled from Claude Opus 4.5 — high-reasoning GGUF
why_it_matters: A 14B model distilled from Claude Opus 4.5 for high-reasoning tasks, available in GGUF format for local inference. Ironic timing given Anthropic's anti-distillation crackdown — and practically useful if you need Opus-level reasoning on consumer hardware.

title: Truth in the time of Artifice
why_it_matters: Latent Space explores the epistemological crisis emerging as AI-generated content becomes indistinguishable from human-created content. Timely given the deepfake and AI-detection debates accelerating this week.
